_description: Technician Productivity — KPI definitions, benchmark targets, report structure, period types

billed_hours.label: Billed Hours
billed_hours.definition: Total labor hours invoiced to customers in the period.
billed_hours.note: 8-hour shift; 6.5 billed is strong. Below 5 indicates inefficiency or scheduling gaps.

efficiency_pct.label: Efficiency %
efficiency_pct.definition: Billed hours divided by clocked hours, expressed as a percentage.
efficiency_pct.note: 100% = tech bills exactly what they clock. 120%+ means they beat flat-rate time. Below 80% needs coaching.

productivity_pct.label: Productivity %
productivity_pct.definition: Billed hours divided by available hours (scheduled shift hours).
productivity_pct.note: Accounts for non-billable time (training, comebacks, shop duties).

revenue_per_tech.label: Revenue per Technician
revenue_per_tech.definition: Total labor revenue attributed to each technician in the period.
revenue_per_tech.note: At $120/hr labor rate × 6.5 billed hours = $780/day target.

comeback_rate.label: Comeback Rate
comeback_rate.definition: Percentage of repairs returned within 30 days for the same complaint.
comeback_rate.note: Industry acceptable is under 2%. Above 5% requires quality process review.

average_repair_order.label: Average Repair Order (ARO)
average_repair_order.definition: Total revenue divided by number of repair orders.
average_repair_order.note: Low ARO may indicate undertelling or failing to present inspection findings.

inspection_presentation_rate.label: Inspection Presentation Rate
inspection_presentation_rate.definition: Percentage of MPI findings presented to the customer vs. total MPI items found.
inspection_presentation_rate.note: If techs find issues but advisors don't present them, revenue is left on the table.

labor_rate_defaults.note: Use shop's actual rate from shop_profile.json if available.

coaching_triggers.note: Flag these in the report summary so the manager sees them immediately.
